perturbation.original_text: In connection with the consideration of a possible transaction involving RHI and JDA (a “ Possible Transaction ”), the Party referred to as the “ Provider ” is prepared to make available to the Party referred to as the “ Recipient ” certain “Evaluation Material” (as defined in Section 2 below) in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with the consideration of a possible transaction involving RHI and JDA (a “ Possible Transaction ”), the Party referred to as the “ Discloser ” is prepared to make available to the Party referred to as the “ Recipient ” certain “Evaluation Material” (as defined in Section 2 below) in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Provider" to "Discloser", the contract introduces ambiguity because the term "Provider" is used consistently throughout the agreement. This contradicts the general understanding established in the contract and will create in-text contradictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions . (a) The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder, The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s or its Representatives possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) was or is independently developed by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives without the use of or reference to any Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Provider" to "Discloser" introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text consistently refers to the 'Provider' in connection with providing evaluation material, and the contradicted text explicitly defines 'Evaluation Material' as information concerning the 'Provider'. By changing 'Provider' to 'Discloser' in the changed text, the contract now uses 'Discloser' in a way that creates ambiguity, because the contradicted definition still refers to 'Provider'. This misalignment directly contradicts the existing definition and creates uncertainty about who is furnishing the Evaluation Material and to whom the definition applies, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder,
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Originator which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder,
perturbation.explanation: Changing Provider to Originator within the definition of Evaluation Material causes confusion because 'Provider' is used consistently elsewhere. The phrase 'information furnished by the Provider hereunder' now contradicts the initial part of the sentence, raising questions about whose information is being evaluated. This introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions . (a) The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Originator which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder, The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s or its Representatives possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) was or is independently developed by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives without the use of or reference to any Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Provider' with 'Originator' in the definition of 'Evaluation Material'. However, the phrase 'information furnished by the Provider hereunder' remains in the same definition. The contradicted text is the same changed text. This creates a contradiction within the definition itself, as the information is supposed to be furnished by the 'Originator' according to the first part of the sentence, but then it is stated to be furnished by the 'Provider' in the second part. This causes confusion about whose information is being referred to and whose obligations apply, which is a strong contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient and its Representatives will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and, subject to Section 5, will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives for the purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient and its Representatives will use the Assessment Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and, subject to Section 5, will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives for the purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of "Assessment Material" while keeping Evaluation Material in the same sentence creates confusion. Because the definition of Evaluation Material remains unchanged, the use of Assessment Material introduces ambiguity and a contradiction. This in-text contradiction within Section 3 raises questions about the scope of permitted usage and disclosure. It creates uncertainty and affect the interpretation of the agreement. The term Assessment Material will create an in-text contradiction in this same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Evaluation Material . The Recipient and its Representatives will use the Assessment Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and, subject to Section 5, will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives for the purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. The Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of the Recipient’s Representatives, other than those of Recipient’s unaffiliated Representatives who have entered into a separate confidentiality agreement with the Provider. This Agreement does not grant the Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein. For the avoidance of doubt, RHI agrees that it shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of JDA to any of its affiliates that are not included in the definition of RHI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces "Assessment Material" into Section 3, while simultaneously referring to "Evaluation Material." The explanation states this creates an in-text contradiction because the unchanged definition of Evaluation Material clashes with the new mention of Assessment Material, creating ambiguity regarding usage and disclosure. The contradicted text reiterates this issue, showcasing the conflict caused by the simultaneous use of both terms within the same section. The 'Misaligned Terminology' definition clearly applies here, as the contract now uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the foregoing, RHI agrees that neither RHI nor any Representative (to the extent acting on behalf and at the direction of RHI) of RHI will, without the prior written consent of JDA, enter into any exclusive arrangement with respect to the provision of debt financing in connection with a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the foregoing, RHI agrees that neither RHI nor any Representative (to the extent acting on behalf and at the direction of RHI) of RHI will, without the prior verbal consent of JDA, enter into any exclusive arrangement with respect to the provision of debt financing in connection with a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'prior written consent' to 'prior verbal consent' creates an in-text contradiction because Section 15, which stipulates that 'No provision of this Agreement can be waived or amended in favor of either Party except by written consent of the other Party'. Therefore, requiring only verbal consent directly contradicts the written consent requirement specified in Section 15. This introduces an in-text contradiction that affects the enforceability of this financing arrangement. This contradiction will be in Section 15 of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Modifications and Waiver . No provision of this Agreement can be waived or amended in favor of either Party except by written consent of the other Party, which consent shall specifically refer to such provision and explicitly make such waiver or amendment. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'prior written consent' to 'prior verbal consent'. The contradicted text explicitly states 'No provision of this Agreement can be waived or amended in favor of either Party except by written consent of the other Party'. Thus, the changed text introduces a direct conflict with the contradicted text, as it allows for verbal consent where written consent is explicitly required, which falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Documents.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Evaluation Material" to "Documents" introduces ambiguity and a contradiction, especially given that Section 2 specifically defines "Evaluation Material". It's unclear if the obligations now extend to all documents or just the defined "Evaluation Material." It will also create an in-text contradiction and uncertainty regarding the persistence of obligations, particularly after material has been returned or destroyed as that it the main topic on the initial sentence. This will lead to uncertainty regarding the definition in Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions . (a) The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder, The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s or its Representatives possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) was or is independently developed by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives without the use of or reference to any Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Evaluation Material" with "Documents." The contradicted text defines "Evaluation Material" specifically. By broadening the term to "Documents," the changed text creates a contradiction because the obligations regarding "Documents" are no longer clearly limited to the defined "Evaluation Material" as intended by the original agreement, thus creating ambiguity and potentially conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient and its Representatives agree not to use any Evaluation Material of the Provider in violation of applicable securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient and its Representatives agree not to use any Confidential Information of the Provider in violation of applicable securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Evaluation Material" to "Confidential Information" creates an in-text contradiction because "Evaluation Material" is specifically defined in Section 2. Now the same section is being reffered as Confidential Information. The agreement lacks a definition of “Confidential Information”, the text loses specificity. This introduces ambiguity and affects the clarity of prohibited uses, which can create a contradiction, specially on the obligations of the parties. This will raise uncertainty about which type of information is covered under the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions . (a) The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder, The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s or its Representatives possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) was or is independently developed by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives without the use of or reference to any Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Evaluation Material,' which is a defined term in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Material' with 'Confidential Information,' a term not defined within the contract. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract now uses a term ('Confidential Information') without specifying its meaning, while simultaneously having a defined term ('Evaluation Material') that is intended to apply to the same information. The change introduces ambiguity and potential for misinterpretation, directly conflicting with the clear definition of 'Evaluation Material' elsewhere in the document, as described in the explanation provided, leading to a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
